steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: f(x) = 26(1.20)^(x/3). We need the percent increase when x increases by 6. A 6-unit increase adds 6/3 = 2 to the exponent, so f is multiplied by (1.20)² = 1.44. The percent increase p satisfies 1 + p/100 = 1.44, so p = 44.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘20’: 20% is the growth factor per 3 units, not per 6. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘31’: No clean derivation; likely a misremembered approximation. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘40’: Doubling 20 ignores compounding (20 + 20 = 40, but the correct answer compounds). ✗
  • Choice D — ‘44’: (1.20)² = 1.44, a 44% increase. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
